content.text: Freshness and Completeness Checks for RAG Sources

A RAG knowledge base is only as reliable as its most stale or incomplete document. Freshness checks detect when source content has aged beyond its acceptable window; completeness checks verify that every ingested document provides sufficient structure and field coverage for accurate retrieval. Together they form an automated quality gate that either blocks degraded content from reaching the retriever or triggers re-ingestion before users are impacted.
Freshness measures the gap between when source content last changed and when the knowledge base reflects that change. Three sub-dimensions matter independently.
Source Freshness Thresholds by Content Class
Regulations change and must be current for legal advice
Policies drift slowly; stale copy causes compliance risk
Feature docs become misleading after releases
High volatility; stale data drives wrong decisions

from __future__ import annotations
import enum
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

class FreshnessStatus(enum.Enum):
    FRESH   = "fresh"
    WARN    = "warn"
    STALE   = "stale"    # block from retrieval; queue re-ingest
    MISSING = "missing"  # no timestamp recorded at all

# Per-class thresholds in hours
FRESHNESS_THRESHOLDS: dict[str, dict[str, float]] = {
    "regulatory":  {"warn": 24,  "stale": 48},
    "policy":      {"warn": 168, "stale": 336},    # 7 d / 14 d
    "product_doc": {"warn": 720, "stale": 1440},   # 30 d / 60 d
    "research":    {"warn": 2160,"stale": 4320},   # 90 d / 180 d
    "market_data": {"warn": 1,   "stale": 4},
    "default":     {"warn": 168, "stale": 336},
}

@dataclass
class FreshnessResult:
    doc_id: str
    content_class: str
    source_updated_at: Optional[datetime]
    ingested_at: Optional[datetime]
    embedding_created_at: Optional[datetime]
    source_age_hours: Optional[float]
    ingestion_lag_hours: Optional[float]
    status: FreshnessStatus
    violations: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)

def _age_hours(ts: Optional[datetime]) -> Optional[float]:
    if ts is None:
        return None
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    if ts.tzinfo is None:
        ts = ts.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    return (now - ts).total_seconds() / 3600

def compute_freshness(
    doc_id: str,
    content_class: str,
    source_updated_at: Optional[datetime],
    ingested_at: Optional[datetime],
    embedding_created_at: Optional[datetime],
) -> FreshnessResult:
    thresholds = FRESHNESS_THRESHOLDS.get(content_class, FRESHNESS_THRESHOLDS["default"])
    source_age = _age_hours(source_updated_at)
    lag = (
        (ingested_at - source_updated_at).total_seconds() / 3600
        if ingested_at and source_updated_at else None
    )
    violations: list[str] = []
    status = FreshnessStatus.FRESH

    if source_age is None:
        violations.append("source_updated_at missing")
        return FreshnessResult(
            doc_id, content_class, source_updated_at, ingested_at,
            embedding_created_at, None, lag, FreshnessStatus.MISSING, violations
        )

    if source_age > thresholds["stale"]:
        status = FreshnessStatus.STALE
        violations.append(
            f"source age {source_age:.1f}h exceeds stale threshold {thresholds['stale']}h"
        )
    elif source_age > thresholds["warn"]:
        status = FreshnessStatus.WARN
        violations.append(
            f"source age {source_age:.1f}h exceeds warn threshold {thresholds['warn']}h"
        )

    if lag is not None and lag > 4:   # universal ingestion lag SLA: 4 h
        violations.append(f"ingestion lag {lag:.1f}h > 4h SLA")
        if status == FreshnessStatus.FRESH:
            status = FreshnessStatus.WARN

    return FreshnessResult(
        doc_id, content_class, source_updated_at, ingested_at,
        embedding_created_at, source_age, lag, status, violations
    )
Completeness verifies that each document provides enough metadata and content for the retriever to operate correctly. It has three sub-dimensions: field presence, chunk count, and character coverage.

from __future__ import annotations
import enum
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any

# Minimum metadata fields every chunk in the vector store must carry
REQUIRED_CHUNK_FIELDS: set[str] = {
    "doc_id",
    "chunk_id",
    "content_class",
    "source_updated_at",
    "ingested_at",
    "classification_label",   # data classification (public / internal / confidential)
    "permitted_apps",         # list of app IDs allowed to retrieve this chunk
    "embedding_model",
}

OPTIONAL_BUT_SCORED_FIELDS: dict[str, float] = {
    # field name → contribution to completeness score when present
    "source_url":          0.10,
    "document_title":      0.10,
    "author":              0.05,
    "version":             0.05,
    "jurisdiction":        0.10,   # important for regulatory content
    "review_cycle_days":   0.05,
}

@dataclass
class FieldCompletenessResult:
    doc_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    missing_required: list[str]
    optional_score: float        # 0.0 – 1.0 weighted coverage of optional fields
    passes: bool                 # True iff missing_required is empty

def check_field_completeness(
    chunk: dict[str, Any]
) -> FieldCompletenessResult:
    missing = [f for f in REQUIRED_CHUNK_FIELDS if not chunk.get(f)]
    opt_total = sum(OPTIONAL_BUT_SCORED_FIELDS.values())
    opt_earned = sum(
        w for f, w in OPTIONAL_BUT_SCORED_FIELDS.items() if chunk.get(f)
    )
    return FieldCompletenessResult(
        doc_id=chunk.get("doc_id", "<unknown>"),
        chunk_id=chunk.get("chunk_id", "<unknown>"),
        missing_required=missing,
        optional_score=opt_earned / opt_total,
        passes=len(missing) == 0,
    )

Beyond individual field presence, a document must produce a minimum number of retrievable chunks and cover a sufficient fraction of the source text. Short or truncated documents produce artificially high retrieval precision on sparse content while silently omitting material the user would expect.

from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass

# Minimum chunks a document must produce after splitting
MIN_CHUNKS_BY_CLASS: dict[str, int] = {
    "regulatory":  3,
    "policy":      2,
    "product_doc": 2,
    "research":    5,
    "market_data": 1,
    "default":     2,
}

# Fraction of source character count that must appear in chunks
MIN_CHAR_COVERAGE: float = 0.80

@dataclass
class ChunkCompletenessResult:
    doc_id: str
    content_class: str
    actual_chunks: int
    expected_min_chunks: int
    source_char_count: int
    indexed_char_count: int
    char_coverage: float
    passes: bool
    violations: list[str]

def check_chunk_completeness(
    doc_id: str,
    content_class: str,
    actual_chunks: int,
    source_char_count: int,
    indexed_char_count: int,
) -> ChunkCompletenessResult:
    min_chunks = MIN_CHUNKS_BY_CLASS.get(content_class, MIN_CHUNKS_BY_CLASS["default"])
    coverage = indexed_char_count / source_char_count if source_char_count > 0 else 0.0

    violations: list[str] = []
    if actual_chunks < min_chunks:
        violations.append(
            f"chunk count {actual_chunks} < minimum {min_chunks} for class '{content_class}'"
        )
    if coverage < MIN_CHAR_COVERAGE:
        violations.append(
            f"character coverage {coverage:.0%} < {MIN_CHAR_COVERAGE:.0%} threshold"
        )

    return ChunkCompletenessResult(
        doc_id=doc_id,
        content_class=content_class,
        actual_chunks=actual_chunks,
        expected_min_chunks=min_chunks,
        source_char_count=source_char_count,
        indexed_char_count=indexed_char_count,
        char_coverage=coverage,
        passes=len(violations) == 0,
        violations=violations,
    )
Combined Quality Report and Gate Decision

A scheduled job runs the full check pipeline against the source catalog. Critical content classes run hourly; standard content runs daily. The runner posts results to a quality event bus that downstream systems consume to trigger re-ingestion or paging.

from __future__ import annotations
import json
import logging
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from dataclasses import asdict
from typing import Callable, Iterable

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

@dataclass
class SourceCatalogEntry:
    doc_id: str
    content_class: str
    source_updated_at: Optional[datetime]
    ingested_at: Optional[datetime]
    embedding_created_at: Optional[datetime]
    chunk_ids: list[str]
    source_char_count: int
    indexed_char_count: int

def run_freshness_completeness_scan(
    catalog: Iterable[SourceCatalogEntry],
    fetch_chunks: Callable[[str], list[dict]],
    event_sink: Callable[[dict], None],
    *,
    only_content_classes: Optional[set[str]] = None,
) -> dict:
    results: dict[str, DocumentQualityStatus] = {}
    block_count = warn_count = pass_count = 0

    for entry in catalog:
        if only_content_classes and entry.content_class not in only_content_classes:
            continue

        freshness = compute_freshness(
            entry.doc_id,
            entry.content_class,
            entry.source_updated_at,
            entry.ingested_at,
            entry.embedding_created_at,
        )

        chunks = fetch_chunks(entry.doc_id)
        field_results = [check_field_completeness(c) for c in chunks]
        worst_field = next(
            (r for r in field_results if not r.passes),
            field_results[0] if field_results else None,
        )

        chunk_result = check_chunk_completeness(
            entry.doc_id,
            entry.content_class,
            actual_chunks=len(chunks),
            source_char_count=entry.source_char_count,
            indexed_char_count=entry.indexed_char_count,
        )

        if worst_field is None:
            # No chunks at all → treat as incomplete
            from dataclasses import field as dc_field
            worst_field = FieldCompletenessResult(
                entry.doc_id, "<none>",
                list(REQUIRED_CHUNK_FIELDS), 0.0, False
            )

        report = build_document_quality_report(freshness, worst_field, chunk_result)
        results[entry.doc_id] = report.status

        event_sink({
            "event": "document_quality_check",
            "doc_id": entry.doc_id,
            "status": report.status.value,
            "violations": report.all_violations,
            "blocks_retrieval": report.blocks_retrieval(),
            "ts": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        })

        if report.blocks_retrieval():
            block_count += 1
            if report.requires_oncall():
                log.error("CRITICAL quality failure — paging oncall: %s", entry.doc_id)
            else:
                log.warning("Quality block: %s status=%s", entry.doc_id, report.status.value)
        elif report.status == DocumentQualityStatus.WARN:
            warn_count += 1
        else:
            pass_count += 1

    return {
        "scanned": len(results),
        "pass": pass_count,
        "warn": warn_count,
        "blocked": block_count,
        "ts": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    }
Enforcing the Gate in the Retriever
The quality status for each document is written to a fast lookup store (Redis hash or in-memory dict, refreshed on every scan). The retriever checks status before including any chunk in a response.
Hourly for market_data / regulatory; daily for everything else; event-triggered on new ingestion
Prefer authoritative source API timestamps over filesystem mtime; document which field is canonical in metadata schema
Treat missing source_updated_at as INCOMPLETE rather than FRESH — defaulting to pass silently hides data quality problems
Each re-ingest must update source_updated_at and ingested_at; otherwise repeated checks always re-queue the same documents
Store status at the document level; applying per-chunk overrides adds retriever complexity without proportional benefit
Start permissive (warn-only) for 30 days while baseline distributions are collected; tighten thresholds based on P95 source age by content class
Monitor block rate weekly; > 5% blocked documents typically indicates missing timestamp propagation in the ingestion pipeline
Quality failures must block retrieval, not just log. Logging-only freshness checks give a false sense of coverage.
Using wall-clock scan time as source_updated_at.
The scan timestamp is not the source timestamp. Conflating them makes every re-scan reset the freshness clock.
Skipping completeness checks for small documents.
Short docs with few fields are the most likely to be truncated or mis-ingested; completeness checks are most valuable there.
Thresholds must be externally configurable so stewards can tighten them without a deployment.

Freshness checks catch documents whose source content has been updated but whose knowledge-base representation is stale. Completeness checks verify that every chunk carries the required metadata fields and sufficient text coverage. The combined quality gate — enforced both in a scheduled scanner and inline in the retriever — prevents degraded content from reaching users while automatically triggering re-ingestion so the issue self-heals without manual intervention.
